data.content_ref: inline:The account claims Egypt survived because it was a 'net grain exporter and core member, not a dependent,' yet the mechanism describes the collapse as triggered by disruption of the great powers' gift-exchange network when 'the Hittite-Egyptian rivalry ended with the peace treaty' and 'internal Hittite succession crises severed the flow of prestige goods and emergency grain shipments.' If the network's stabilizing function depended on competitive reciprocity among great powers, Egypt's survival as a core member requires explanation: why did the same network breakdown that supposedly crippled Hatti not equally destabilize Egypt's own administrative and economic structures? The account provides no mechanism by which Egypt was insulated from the very network failure it posits as the cause of collapse elsewhere.
